mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across consumer discretionary and fixed investment channels, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month when the economy operates above potential output.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of observations during high-capacity periods, or the signal reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 70% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal loses predictive power when real yields are already elevated.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms genuine labor market tightening, supporting sustained corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth rates directly correlate with aggregate corporate profit expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week as markets price in improved macroeconomic fundamentals.
failure_condition: Walk-forward analysis shows no significant positive correlation between GDPC1 growth and 5-day SPX returns, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in the industrial production index signal a contraction in manufacturing activity and supply-side weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in > 60% of double-dip INDPRO months, or the signal fails to maintain predictive accuracy when monetary policy is simultaneously easing.
